About

Cases explained through the system applied

The cases are written as operating patterns. The point is to show how the change happens, not to inflate the result.

Review the system See the process
01

Context

Case overview

Each case starts with the operating problem, shows the system layer applied and ends with the change that became visible.

02

Friction

Problem snapshots

The recurring case themes are structural, not cosmetic.

CRM stages do not match the buying motion
Prospecting runs without reliable routing
Signals are visible but not actionable
Reporting cannot explain pipeline movement
03

System layer

System applied

The work usually combines several layers because one isolated fix rarely holds.

Redefine the model

Rebuild objects, stages and required inputs around one commercial reality.

Connect workflows

Move routing, tasking and ownership through explicit rules.

Sync data and signals

Feed enrichment and engagement into priority and action.

Rebuild reporting

Make dashboards read the same structure used by the team.

04

Proof

Results highlights

The result is that the commercial system becomes usable in live operating reviews.

CRM-based pipeline reviews
One stage language across teams
Signal-led prioritization
Less manual reporting work
05

Operating model

Key insights

The same lessons show up repeatedly across implementations.

Tool change is not enough
Ownership makes workflow design real
Data structure decides reporting quality
Revenue systems need review loops

Case grid

Case

Problème: Pipeline reviews depended on spreadsheet explanations because HubSpot stages and fields did not match the real buying motion.

Système: Lifecycle rules, stage criteria, required properties and dashboards were rebuilt around one commercial definition.

Résultat: Pipeline movement became reviewable inside the CRM and forecast conversations stopped relying on manual reconciliation.

Case

Problème: Prospecting depended on manual interpretation of account fit, timing and follow-up ownership.

Système: Targeting rules, sequences, CRM handoffs and review cadence were redesigned as one connected motion.

Résultat: Outbound became easier to review, route and improve because replies, owners and next steps followed the same model.

Case

Problème: Sales could see account activity but could not convert it into clear priority and action inside the CRM.

Système: Signals, enrichment, scoring and meeting workflow were connected so timing changed tasks, queues and follow-up.

Résultat: Prioritization became visible and repeatable instead of depending on rep intuition or disconnected tools.

Review a similar case

Consulter

Rotata turns fragmented revenue operations into one operating model that marketing and sales can read, run and improve.
